description: Make package.json the single source of truth for plugin version. Remove the abstract `version` property from OpenTabsPlugin, inject the version from package.json at IIFE build time, and remove all manual `readonly version` declarations from plugin classes.

userStories.title: Remove abstract version from OpenTabsPlugin and make it a concrete mutable property
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the SDK to not require me to declare a version property so that the version comes from package.json automatically at build time.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenTabsPlugin no longer declares `abstract readonly version: string`
OpenTabsPlugin declares `version` as a concrete property with a default value (e.g., `version = '0.0.0'`) so it can be overwritten by the IIFE wrapper at build time
The property must NOT be readonly — the IIFE wrapper needs to assign to it after instantiation
TypeScript compilation succeeds — plugins that still declare `readonly version` continue to compile (override of a non-abstract mutable property is allowed)
userStories.notes: File: `platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts` line 194. Change `abstract readonly version: string;` to `version: string = '0.0.0';` (non-abstract, non-readonly, with default). The default '0.0.0' is a fallback for plugins that haven't been rebuilt yet — it will be overwritten by the IIFE wrapper in US-002. The JSDoc comment on line 193 (`/** Semver version string */`) should be updated to say the version is injected from package.json at build time. Existing plugins that still have `readonly version = '0.0.1'` will compile fine because TypeScript allows overriding a mutable base property with readonly in a subclass.

userStories.title: Inject package.json version into the IIFE wrapper at build time
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want `opentabs-plugin build` to inject my package.json version into the adapter IIFE so that the runtime version always matches the package version.
acceptanceCriteria: The IIFE wrapper code in bundleIIFE sets `plugin.version` to the package.json version after the plugin instance is imported
The version is injected as a string literal via esbuild `define` or directly in the wrapper code template

The validatePlugin function in build.ts no longer validates plugin.version from the class instance — it validates pkgJson.version instead (or removes the version validation entirely since package.json version is already validated by parsePluginPackageJson)
After building a plugin, the adapter IIFE reports the correct package.json version when accessed via globalThis.__openTabs.adapters[name].version
userStories.notes:
File: `platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts`.

1. In `bundleIIFE()` (line 712): The function signature is `bundleIIFE(sourceEntry, outDir, pluginName)`. It needs a new parameter for the package version. The caller at ~line 1090 has access to `pkgJson.version`. Pass it through.

2. In the IIFE wrapper code (starts line 720): After `currentAdapters[${name}] = plugin;` (line 857), add: `plugin.version = ${JSON.stringify(pkgVersion)};` where `pkgVersion` is the new parameter. This must be BEFORE the hashAndFreeze snippet runs (which is appended later in the build pipeline at line 1124), since hashAndFreeze calls Object.freeze(a) on the adapter. The version assignment inside the wrapper code happens at IIFE execution time, before freeze.

3. In `validatePlugin()` (line 313): Remove the version validation block at lines 320-327 (`if (plugin.version.length === 0) ... `). The package.json version is already validated by `parsePluginPackageJson()` in the shared package (`platform/shared/src/manifest.ts` lines 65-69) which is called via `validatePackageJson()` at line 1004. No need for double validation. If you prefer to keep a cross-check, validate `pkgJson.version` instead of `plugin.version`.

4. The `bundleIIFE` call site is around line 1090. Search for `await bundleIIFE(` to find it. Pass `pkgJson.version` as the new argument.

userStories.title: Remove readonly version from plugin classes and scaffold template
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want new plugins to not include a version property in the class so that there is no confusion about where the version comes from.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts generatePluginIndex function no longer includes `readonly version = '0.0.1'` in the generated class template
All plugins still compile and build successfully
userStories.notes:
Three files to edit:

1. `plugins/slack/src/index.ts` line 29: Remove the entire line `readonly version = '0.0.1';`
2. `plugins/e2e-test/src/index.ts` line 74: Remove the entire line `readonly version = '0.0.1';`
3. `platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts` line 357: Remove the line `  readonly version = '0.0.1';` from the template string in `generatePluginIndex()`.

After removing these, the plugins will inherit the default `version = '0.0.0'` from the base class (set in US-001). At build time, the IIFE wrapper (set in US-002) will overwrite it with the package.json version.

Also check tests:
- `platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts` or `platform/create-plugin/src/index.test.ts` may assert on the generated plugin class containing `readonly version`. Update those assertions to not expect the version line.
- `platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts` tests may validate `plugin.version` — update if needed after US-002 changes.

userStories.title: Update tests for version injection changes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all unit tests to pass after the version source-of-truth change so that CI is green.
acceptanceCriteria: All tests in platform/plugin-tools/ pass (build command tests no longer expect class-level version validation)
All tests in platform/cli/ pass (scaffold tests updated for no version line)
All tests in platform/browser-extension/ pass (iife-injection tests, message-router tests still verify version flow)
userStories.notes:
This story is a catch-all for any test updates needed after US-001 through US-003. Key test files to check:

1. `platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.test.ts` — may have tests for validatePlugin that check version validation. Update or remove those tests.
2. `platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts` — may assert generated plugin code contains `readonly version`. Remove that assertion.
3. `platform/create-plugin/src/index.test.ts` — line 332 area may check for version in generated files.
4. `platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.test.ts` — tests for verifyAdapterVersion should still pass since the version verification mechanism itself hasn't changed (just the source of the version value).
5. `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.test.ts` — lines 537-545 test version defaulting; these should still pass.

Run `npm run test` to identify any failures and fix them. Then run `npm run test:e2e` to verify end-to-end.
